title: Dans controls conventional-language monochrome while frontend availability is contextual
summary: C# and Python default to source-faithful monochrome but follow the shared Dans monochrome preference; the C++ frontend remains unavailable from menu contexts outside the owner's explicit C/C++/CUDA suffix set.
details: C# and Python never participate in the C/C++/CUDA spelling frontend, but their Tree-sitter presentation follows the globally remembered dans_monochrome_requested preference rather than being permanently monochrome.

When monochrome is disabled from a C# or Python buffer, language-qualified Tree-sitter captures restore the active colorscheme's ordinary palette. Semantic tokens stay disabled so Tree-sitter remains the single coloring source and LSP attachment cannot silently change presentation.
The C++ frontend may force monochrome only in buffers where that frontend renders. Its enabled state elsewhere does not lock the Monochrome menu row for C# or Python.
The Frontend presentation row is visibly gray and activation is a no-op unless the menu was opened from a .c, .cpp, .h, .hpp, .hh, .cc, .cu, or .cuh source buffer.
Definition navigation, references, refactoring, diagnostics, completion, and source bytes are independent of either presentation choice.
rationale: Monochrome remains the low-noise default while retaining the owner's existing palette control instead of creating a special permanent policy for two languages.
Buffer-context locking reflects the actual capability boundary: a C++ frontend enabled in another window has no presentation authority over Python or C#.
A disabled menu row communicates scope before activation and prevents a global frontend transition from an unrelated source buffer.
alternatives: Keep C# and Python permanently monochrome and independent of Dans; superseded because the owner explicitly expects monochrome disabling through the menu to work.
Allow the frontend row to toggle globally from every filetype; rejected because the owner expects it to be unavailable outside C/C++/CUDA source contexts.
Re-enable LSP semantic-token colors with normal Tree-sitter colors; deferred because the owner requested less visual noise and one syntax-color source is deterministic.

provenance.note: Owner asked whether Python/C# monochrome could be disabled through Dans and specified that the frontend option must be grayed outside .c/.cpp/.h/.hpp/.hh/.cc/.cu/.cuh files.

evidence.claim: Headless interaction verifies the exact enabled suffixes, disabled row appearance and no-op activation, context-sensitive monochrome control, normal palette restoration, source fidelity, and persistent semantic-token suppression.

questions: Can the Dans menu turn monochrome off for Python or C#?
Why is the frontend option gray in a Python buffer?
Which file extensions enable Frontend presentation in the Dans menu?
Does enabling normal C# colors also enable the C++ frontend?

last_verified.method: Owner clarification plus deterministic menu interaction and language-qualified highlight transition coverage.
